marketName: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions

features.name: Mechanical Handle Locking
features.rationale: Core commodity FP function. Safety (9): prevents unauthorized access to collection vaults and conservation storage. Reliability (8): mechanical fail-safe essential in heritage buildings with unreliable mains.

features.name: Audible Alarm Trigger
features.rationale: Commodity FP explicitly includes audible alarm. Safety (9): 95 dB deters theft and alerts adjacent staff. Stress (7): security officers know any unauthorized activation is audible.

features.name: Single-Motion Emergency Release
features.rationale: Regulatory knockout without it. Safety (10): highest safety score; core life-safety function. Skill (8): zero prior knowledge required from museum visitors including reduced-mobility occupants.

features.name: Key Override Bypass
features.rationale: Institutionally required; absence makes product inoperable. Stress (7) and Pain (7): conservation teams and night-shift security access storage without triggering false alerts.

features.name: Battery-Powered Operation
features.rationale: Highest overall fit. Eliminates 3-12 month listed building consent barrier that disqualifies wired incumbent. Pain (9): removes heritage consent barrier. Skill (8): no electrician needed. Delight (7): genuinely surprising.

features.name: Pre-Alarm Warning
features.rationale: Absent in basic alarms; reduces false-alarm fatigue during opening hours. Stress (7): duty managers avoid unnecessary alarm investigations from accidental visitor contact. Safety (4): modest — pre-alarm may give unauthorized users 1-2 seconds warning.

productRelatedNeeds: Minimize the likelihood of 868 MHz signal degradation from heritage-grade masonry construction creating silent monitoring gaps exceeding 15 minutes
Minimize the time required to pre-validate 868 MHz radio coverage through actual building wall materials before committing to gateway placement
Minimize the likelihood of 9V block battery depletion below operational threshold within the 2-year maintenance window in climate-controlled conservation environments
Minimize the time required to achieve complete exit monitoring coverage across all doors in a multi-room heritage building using exclusively surface-mount fixing methods
Minimize the likelihood of EasyWave protocol incompatibility causing integration failure when the museum BMS uses BACnet, KNX, or IP-based protocols
